spec_match.rationale: The demo uses current CSS Text 4 grammar with text-fit: grow and text-fit: shrink, and Chrome 148 shows a clean unsupported fallback for the future v150 syntax.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the resize panel, width slider, short/standard/long presets, custom text input, row text, pressed states, status text, and live measurement readout.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index and page copy now describe grow, shrink, and fixed-size text rather than non-existent contain/scale-down modes.

edge_cases.rationale: Users can now switch between short, standard, long, and custom strings to expose fitting and overflow states at different widths.

references.rationale: The see also section properly links to the chromestatus.com entry for feature 5104141688635392.

design_system.rationale: The page conforms to the design system, utilizing the specified CSS variables and clean typographic layout.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the original gap at http://localhost:3000/v150/css-text-fit-property/text-fit-demo/?verify=before-user-text-input: the page had a working width slider but no editable text controls or presets. The repaired page was tested at /v150/css-text-fit-property/text-fit-demo/?verify=after-user-text-input in Chrome 148: Short, Standard, Long, custom text, 180px, and 620px states updated the resize headline, all three comparison columns, the input value, pressed preset state, status text, and four measurement rows with no console errors and only successful network requests. The feature conformance route ran at /v150/css-text-fit-property/conformance/?verify=text-fit-demo-user-input with 1 PASS / 6 FAIL / 7 TOTAL, expected because Chrome 148 does not yet parse the v150 text-fit syntax; the concept displayed the unsupported fallback cleanly.
